back_translation: The words of Nehemiah son of Hacaliah. In the month of Chislev, in the twentieth year, while I was in the citadel-palace of the city of Susa,

Hanani, one of my brothers, traveled here together with some men from the province of Judah. I questioned them about the Jews who had survived and remained from the captivity, and about the city of Jerusalem.

They answered me, "The survivors who remain from the captivity who are there in that province are in great trouble and disgrace. The wall of the city of Jerusalem has been broken down, and the gates of the city have been burned with fire."
When I heard these words, I sat down and wept, and mourned for many days, both fasting and praying before the God of heaven.
I prayed, saying, "O LORD-Sovereign, God of heaven, the great and awesome God, who keeps covenant and steadfast love toward those who love Him and keep His commandments,

May Your ear be inclined to hear, and Your eyes be open, to listen to the prayer of Your servant which I am praying before Your presence at this time, both day and night, for the people of Israel Your servants, and I confess the sins of the people of Israel which we have committed against You — both I and my father's household have sinned.
We have behaved corruptly against You exceedingly, and have not kept the commandments, statutes, and ordinances which You commanded Moses Your servant.
Please remember the word that You commanded Moses Your servant, saying, 'If you are unfaithful, I will scatter you among the peoples.

But if you return to Me and keep My commandments and do them, even if your scattered ones are at the edge of the sky, I will gather them from there and bring them to the place which I have chosen as a dwelling-place for My name.'
They are Your servants and Your people, whom You redeemed by Your great power and by Your strong hand.

O Sovereign-Master, may Your ear be inclined to hear the prayer of Your servant and the prayer of Your servants who delight to revere Your name. Grant success to Your servant today, and let him receive mercy before this man." At that time I was the cupbearer of the king.
